sections.paragraph: an unused root. Arab. حلك to be black, metaph. to be wretched, unfortunate, like حالك عمر a miserable life. (The primary idea, I judge, is that of burning, scorching, and this root is softened from the Ch. הֲרַךְ, Arab. حرق to scorch, compare חוּם black from the root חוּם and חָמַם.) Hence-
